replies.author: @razgriz3d
replies.text:
​@CultureCasinoor there's this to add to the list along with the seemingly dozens of bills introduced at the state level in the last week to force age verification and censorship/ban people from social media to create a backdoor for more surveillance and digital ID:

Colorado's Senate Bill 26-051 would require operating systems like Windows or Linux to include age verification during initial device setup.

Introduced by Sen. Matt Ball and Rep. Amy Paschal, the bill mandates that OS providers prompt users or parents to enter a birth date or age during account creation.

The bill remains pending, with a committee hearing scheduled for February 24, 2026. 

If passed, the requirements would apply to new device setups starting January 1, 2028

replies.author: @spencerlindhorst5259
replies.text: @CultureCasino potentially. The files show Epstein had consistent correspondence with the highest positions in government, business, and cultural institutions like universities, art, and film(internationally as well). The level of corruption suggests a person had to partake or assist in trafficking and other dark schemes to partake in the benefit. So the main point is people involved with the Epstein class  had to contribute something to gain access—access to money, fame, relationships—essentially being rewarded for doing bad shit. Jared leto is said to be running a cult following on a private island in croatia and is rumored to have been accused of harassment of minors at the very least… sound familiar? And why is jared leto getting lead rolls in blockbuster movies when Hollywood is geared towards profit and leto bombs every movie he’s in? He’s clearly not the most talented actor in America…. Check out the Diddy documentary…. He definitely wasn’t the most talented artist in the industry by far… but he provided an essential service for the elite class that gave him access to fame and wealth.

author: @Slitheringpeanut
text: All I'm seeing is more accusations without proof or a police report.  I'm sorry, but as much as I personally find Leto distasteful, I refuse to be a Leftist and assume accusation mean automatic guilt.  Show us evidence or else it's still all hearsay.  Also, 18 is not underaged in California.  This is starting to smack of another attempt at MeToo.  Either show a police report or I will not believe it.  I still don't like the man, though.  Most of the women who go get a Hollywood job know exactly what to expect, in fact they're the ones who often offer to get a job.

replies.author: @philippeamon7271
replies.text: That's not what it's about - this is primarily concerning the Ukulele Protocol (named for Coleen Ballinger's musical nonpology video), which does have some psychological overlap with age of consent, but it's not the same. Basically, if you are famous/rich/photo model, you are no longer entitled to believe that people who contact you are humans of any inherent agency - they must for all intents and purposes be considered fame leech star fuckers. Their consent is not valid, because they are looking to prostitute themselves/win a rape trial (with or without prior instances of rape). And that absolutely applies to a 17 year old girl, in a culture accustomed to a minimum age of 18 or even 15 for that matter. There are already women, who are so comfortable with this lack of accountability, that they proudly proclaim they weren't old enough to consent when they lost their virginity/desirability at the tender age of 21, and I can guarantee you we will move the goal post again, when society has acclimated to the idea that women must be 25 before having sex, there will be entitled women complaining that they deserve better sex, because they weren't old enough to sell themselves when they were 27, 33 or 48 years old. Because it's always better to deserve better sex, than admitting you don't like it and have to live without the important status and social power and protection (and for women, pregnancy = successful womanhood) that it provides.
